activities_projects.title: The Onion Layer Model
activities_projects.objective: Understand that the Earth is made of several concentric layers
steps: Carefully cut an onion in half
Observe the different layers starting from the center to the outside
Compare these layers to the Crust, Mantle, and Core of the Earth
Draw the cross-section and label the layers
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Visualizes the Earth as a layered structure similar to an onion
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct labeling of the three main layers of the Earth (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Clay Earth Model
activities_projects.objective: Identify the relative thickness of Earth's layers
materials: Red, Yellow, and Brown modeling clay or playdough
steps: Make a small red ball to represent the Inner Core
Wrap a thick layer of yellow clay around it to represent the Mantle
Cover the outside with a very thin layer of brown clay for the Crust
Cut the ball in half to see the internal structure
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes that the crust is the thinnest layer and the mantle is the thickest
activities_projects.evaluation: Representation of the varying thicknesses of the layers (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Sima and Sial Word-Play
activities_projects.objective: Learn the chemical constituents of the continental and oceanic crust
steps: Write 'SI' (Silica) and 'AL' (Alumina) on one card to make SIAL
Write 'SI' (Silica) and 'MA' (Magnesium) on another to make SIMA
Draw a picture of a continent on the SIAL card and an ocean on the SIMA card
Explain which one forms the ocean floor
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Memorizes the main minerals forming the different parts of the Earth's crust
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of minerals in Sial and Sima (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Rock Collection Gallery
activities_projects.objective: Identify the three main types of rocks
materials: Various stones found in the garden or neighborhood
steps: Collect 5-6 different looking rocks
Observe their color, texture, and hardness
Try to group them into Igneous, Sedimentary, or Metamorphic based on pictures in your book
Label your collection in a small box
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Distinguishes between different types of rocks based on physical appearance
activities_projects.evaluation: Attempt to classify rocks correctly with reasons (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Cookie Sedimentary Lesson
activities_projects.objective: Understand how sedimentary rocks are formed in layers
steps: Crush different types of cookies into 'sediments'
Layer them one by one in a clear plastic cup
Press them down firmly and use honey as a 'binder' (cementing)
Observe the layers from the side of the cup
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Explains the process of sedimentation and lithification
activities_projects.evaluation: Successful demonstration of stratified layers (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Melting Wax (Igneous) Magic
activities_projects.objective: Simulate the formation of Igneous rocks from magma
steps: Melt the wax crayons in the bowl (representing magma)
Pour the liquid wax onto a cold surface or into water
Watch it solidify quickly to form a hard mass
Relate this to how volcanic lava turns into rock
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands that igneous rocks are formed by the cooling of molten material
activities_projects.evaluation: Explanation of the transition from liquid to solid state (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Rock Cycle Comic Strip
activities_projects.objective: Trace the transformation of one rock type to another
steps: Draw a 'Magma' character at the start
Show him cooling into an Igneous rock
Show him breaking into Sediments and then becoming a Sedimentary rock
End with him changing into a Metamorphic rock due to heat and pressure
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Visualizes the continuous process of the rock cycle
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical flow of the transformation stages (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Fossil Find Simulation
activities_projects.objective: Learn why fossils are found in sedimentary rocks
materials: A leaf or a plastic toy insect
Plaster of Paris or more clay
steps: Press a leaf into a flat piece of clay to make an impression
Remove the leaf carefully
Cover the impression with another layer of clay or plaster
Peel it back later to see the 'fossil' left behind
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands how remains of plants and animals get trapped in rock layers
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of the fossil impression and explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Mineral Hunt at Home
activities_projects.objective: Identify minerals used in daily life
materials: Household items (Pencil, Salt, Jewelry, Talcum powder)
steps: List 5 items used at home that come from minerals
Find out which mineral they contain (e.g., Graphite in pencils, Gold in jewelry)
Discuss why minerals are important for the human environment
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes that rocks and minerals are essential for our lifestyle
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of the items-minerals list (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Earth's Radius Poster
activities_projects.objective: Memorize key facts and figures about the Earth's interior
steps: Write the number **6371 km** (Earth's radius) in large letters
Write the depth of the deepest mine in the world (**4 km**)
Draw a person digging a hole and label how far they have reached compared to the center
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Realizes the massive scale of the Earth compared to human reach
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct use of facts and figures from the NCERT text (10 marks)
